evidence: BlackLabSnippetParser: 328-line dedicated test class (BlackLabSnippetParserTest) with thorough edge-case coverage — well tested.
LogDiceCalculator: 241-line test class covering boundary values and formula correctness — well tested.
SemanticFieldExplorer: 243-line test using a hand-rolled StubExecutor, testing compare() and exploreByPattern() in isolation — good test isolation strategy.
SimpleTagger: 201-line test — well tested.
PatternSubstitution: 94-line test covering substitution edge cases.
LongIntHashMap: 80-line test.

BlackLabQueryExecutor (545 lines, the core query engine): no pure unit tests. BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and DependencySketchTest exist but require a real index to be useful — they are integration tests without isolation.
ExplorationHandlers (437 lines) and SketchHandlers (456 lines): zero tests — no mock executor, no HTTP handler test.
WordSketchApiServer (182 lines): zero tests — routing, CORS, handler registration, OPTIONS handling all untested.
Main.java CLI argument parsing: zero tests — all command paths untested.
GrammarConfigLoader (552 lines): no direct test file; only RelationsValidationTest (54 lines) which validates a JSON file, not the loader logic.
e2e/, regression/, viz/, indexer/ test directories are empty — planned infrastructure never populated.
No snapshot test overuse — all existing tests are assertion-based unit tests.
No timing-dependent or ordering-sensitive tests observed.
HybridAlgorithmTest (81 lines) and BCQLParserTest (42 lines) provide some parser coverage.

strengths: Pure utility and parsing classes (BlackLabSnippetParser, LogDiceCalculator, PatternSubstitution, LongIntHashMap) are well-covered with focused unit tests.
SemanticFieldExplorer tests use a hand-crafted stub executor to isolate business logic from index access — demonstrates awareness of good test design.
All tests are assertion-based; no snapshot overuse or timing fragility observed.
test_strategy.issue_character: The test suite covers only the pure utility layer; every class that touches HTTP, routing, or index access — which together represent the majority of production-critical code — has zero test coverage.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The bottom third of the stack (parsers, calculators, data structures) is reasonably well tested. But the top two thirds — the HTTP handler layer, the query executor, CLI parsing, and grammar loading — are entirely untested. The empty e2e/ and regression/ directories indicate the gap was recognised but never addressed. Given that BlackLabQueryExecutor (545 lines, the only implementation of the QueryExecutor interface) has no unit tests and the handler classes have no mock-based tests, the coverage of critical paths is very low. This places the codebase below the midpoint for test_strategy.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has no unit tests — the core query engine is entirely untested in isolation
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.java is 545 lines implementing 6 interface methods (findCollocations, executeBcqlQuery, executeSurfacePattern, findDependencyCollocations, executeQuery, getTotalFrequency); no corresponding unit test class exists
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and DependencySketchTest require a real BlackLab index — they are integration tests that cannot run without an index artifact
issues.suggestion: Introduce an interface-level test using a lightweight in-memory Lucene index or a mock BlackLab facade; at minimum test the logDice ranking logic and the collocate extraction methods independently of the index.

issues.summary: ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers have zero tests — HTTP input parsing, validation, and response shape are all untested
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java: 437 lines, 5 handler methods — no test class exists
SketchHandlers.java: 456 lines, 6 handler methods — no test class exists
Input validation logic (missing-param checks, NumberFormatException catches) is exercised by no test
issues.suggestion: Use HttpExchange mocking (e.g., with Mockito or a minimal stub implementing HttpExchange) to test each handler method: verify 400 on missing params, 400 on bad numeric params, 500 on executor failure, and correct JSON shape on success.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader (552 lines) has no dedicated unit test — JSON parsing, relation loading, and alias resolution are untested
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.java is 552 lines; RelationsValidationTest (54 lines) validates only the JSON fixture file format, not the loader methods
getRelation(), getFullPattern(), collocatePosition(), headPosition() — all untested in unit isolation
issues.suggestion: Add GrammarConfigLoaderTest that loads a test fixture JSON and verifies getRelation(), getVersion(), getFullPattern() substitution, and missing-key behaviour.

issues.summary: e2e/, regression/, viz/, indexer/ test directories are empty — integration test infrastructure was created but never populated
evidence: ls of e2e/, regression/, viz/ directories returns no .java files
indexer/ contains only a 'hybrid' subdirectory, also empty
issues.suggestion: Either populate the directories with planned tests or remove them to avoid misleading future contributors about test coverage scope.

root_causes: The codebase was built test-first only for pure logic (parsers, calculators); the HTTP and query layers were built with manual testing against a real index rather than automated unit tests.
BlackLabQueryExecutor's dependency on a real file-system index makes it harder to unit test, but no in-memory test strategy was ever established.
likely_symptoms: ts_empty_test_dirs — symptom of the broader gap, not a root cause
